diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thanks for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Diego. How can I help?
[Caller] I got ripped off. I did over a hundred pulls and the rates are clearly rigged. I want my primogems back.
[Agent] I hear you — that's a lot of pulls, and when luck doesn't go your way, it absolutely can feel like something's off. I take claims like this seriously, so let me pull your actual wish history so we can look at the numbers together. UID please?
[Caller] 606778231.
[Agent] Thank you, David. Let me open your wish log. OK — I see your full history across the last two character banners. You pulled 112 times total. In that span you received two 5-stars — one at pull 78, one at pull 86. You also received seventeen 4-stars, averaging roughly one every 6.6 pulls.
[Caller] Only two 5-stars from 112? That feels awful.
[Agent] I understand, and I want to be straight with you: mathematically, your results are exactly in line with — actually slightly better than — the published rates. The advertised rate for any 5-star on the character banner is 0.6% base, about 1.6% including the soft pity curve, which works out to roughly one 5-star every 62 to 80 pulls on average. You hit two in 112 pulls, which is completely normal and slightly above average. The 4-star rate is 5.1%, you landed 15.2%. Nothing is rigged — you're getting what the rates promise.
[Caller] Oh. I guess I just focused on the disappointments, not the hits.
[Agent] That's a very human thing to do, and honestly, the pull animation is designed to make every non-5-star feel like a near-miss. Let me offer two things. One — I'll send you a summary of your actual vs. expected rates so you can see the math any time doubt creeps in. Two — a free resource: the community has a tool called Paimon.moe that lets you log wishes and see your lifetime luck. Helpful for perspective.
[Caller] That would actually help a lot.
[Agent] Also, per policy, primogems spent on wishes aren't refundable since the pulls completed as expected — but I hope walking through the numbers reassured you. Anything else?
[Caller] No, I feel better. Thanks for not just brushing me off.
[Agent] Never. Take care, David.
